cot_prefix: Okay, let's tackle this question. The user is asking what the International Olympic Committee (IOC) was concerned about regarding the Olympic medals. First, I'll need to refer back to the provided passage. Let me scan through the text again to find the relevant part. The question specifically mentions the IOC's concerns when the jade ring design was proposed. The passage states: "Experts from the International Olympic Committee (IOC) were concerned about the risk of fragility when the jade ring design was proposed." Then it goes on to say the IOC required the jade rings to be strong enough to withstand a 2-meter drop test, and the Shanghai Mint raised it to 3 meters. The medals can survive a 3-meter drop with only minor cracks on the jade rings. So the key point here is the concern about the fragility of the jade rings. The IOC was worried that jade, being a material, might break easily. The mention of fragility and the drop tests directly points to their concern being about the jade's durability. Looking at the answer options: (A) the medals will be worth less – The passage doesn't mention anything about the monetary value or the worth of the medals. So this is probably not correct. (B) the medals are too difficult to make – While the text does mention that the technology is not easy and involves a painstaking manual process, the IOC's specific concern was not about the difficulty of manufacturing but about the risk of fragility. So B is not the right choice. (C) competitors don't like the design – There's no mention of competitors' opinions or dissatisfaction with the design. So C can be ruled out. (D) jade is fragile and easy to break up – This matches exactly with the passage's statement that the IOC was concerned about the risk of fragility. The drop tests were to ensure the jade could handle being dropped without breaking. So D is the correct answer. I think D is the answer here.
